title: paspalum cordaense
intro: Paspalum cordaense, a type of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions, is a member of the Poaceae family and is also known as buffalo grass. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Paspalum cordaense include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Paspalum cordaense can be prepared as a tea or infused in water to be consumed as a herbal remedy, or dried and used as a powder in traditional medicine.
